category_title: Elementary students with emotional/behavioral disorders who yell and throw objects
scenario_title: Independent practice with difficult math, reading, or writing worksheets
minimal_context: Malcolm is assigned an independent math worksheet at his desk in the pull-out special education classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Here is your math worksheet. Please complete these problems at your desk by yourself.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stands up and walks away from the desk) This is stupid. Hey, your drawing looks like garbage!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Malcolm, please go back to your desk and work on your math.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't know how to do this! Come help me right now!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just try your best on your own. I need to check on the others right now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Throws his pencil across the room and drops to the ground) NO! I'm not doing it!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Here is your math worksheet. Please complete these problems at your desk by yourself.

STUDENT: (Stands up and walks away from the desk) This is stupid. Hey, your drawing looks like garbage!

TUTOR: Malcolm, please go back to your desk and work on your math.

STUDENT: I don't know how to do this! Come help me right now!

TUTOR: Just try your best on your own. I need to check on the others right now.

STUDENT: (Throws his pencil across the room and drops to the ground) NO! I'm not doing it!

task.content: Independent assignments (math/reading/writing worksheets) that the student, with assistance, was unlikely to complete with 50% or better accuracy

context.environment: Third-grade pull-out special education classroom during instructional time
context.materials: Math, reading, or writing worksheets; timer worn by teacher
context.social_setting: Independent work with absent teacher attention

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When presented with difficult tasks and teacher attention is absent
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Yelling during class, throwing objects, rolling on the floor
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Attempting to escape and avoid difficult tasks due to repeated failure experienced across schooling
related_quotes: displayed clear patterns of escaping difficult tasks when teacher attention was absent

behavior_patterns.condition: During instruction time when teacher attention is absent
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Wandering the classroom during instruction time, and making rude comments to other students
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Attempting to engage the teacher for attention and assistance potentially lessens the aversive nature of the task (i.e., escape)
related_quotes: when presented with difficult tasks, both students engaged in problem behavior to avoid the work
